mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, tight labor market conditions trigger wage-driven inflation expectations that force the Federal Reserve to accelerate policy normalization, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and contraction in real retail sales indicates supply-side bottlenecks outpacing consumer demand, which squeezes corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative forward returns in at least 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of declining housing starts on consumer wealth and credit availability, leading to sharply negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in tightening periods do not show a statistically significant negative relationship with housing starts, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP per capita expands the total addressable market for corporate revenues, directly supporting earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in GDP expansion phases are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, persistent inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces equity risk premiums higher, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or predictive rank correlation drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with stagnant retail sales indicates labor income is not translating into consumption, signaling a decoupling that precedes earnings revisions downward and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative forward returns in at least 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-capacity utilization environment, any contraction in industrial production triggers supply chain disruptions that spike input costs and compress margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline when industrial production contracts during high utilization, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS.
